docs.title: Agentic RAG Workflows
docs.text: An AI architecture where a reasoning agent autonomously decides when and how to retrieve external knowledge to answer queries, rather than doing a single static lookup. The agent can issue multiple retrieval calls, re-rank results, and synthesize a grounded response.

docs.title: Core Components
docs.text:
1. Orchestration: Using Azure AI Services or LangChain to manage state.
  2. Retrieval: Vectorizing data from the Lakehouse Gold layer into an Azure AI Search index.
  3. Generation: GPT-4o model context injection for precise, grounded answers.

docs.title: Architectural Flow
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[User Query] --> B[Orchestrator]
    B --> C[Vector Database]
    C --> B
    B --> D[LLM (GPT)]
    D --> E[Final Response]

docs.title: Delta Lake
docs.text: An open-source storage layer on top of Parquet files that adds ACID transactions, schema enforcement, time travel, and CDC (Change Data Capture) capabilities to data lakes.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Description ACID Atomic commits — partial writes never visible Time Travel Query historical snapshots Schema Enforcement Rejects bad-schema writes OPTIMIZE/ZORDER File compaction + data skipping

docs.title: Time Travel Example
docs.text:
# Read as of a specific version
df = spark.read.format("delta") \
    .option("versionAsOf", 5) \
    .load("/Tables/silver_sales")

# Restore table to version
from delta.tables import DeltaTable
dt = DeltaTable.forPath(spark, "/Tables/silver_sales")
dt.restoreToVersion(5)

docs.title: OPTIMIZE + Z-ORDER
docs.text:
OPTIMIZE silver_sales
ZORDER BY (customer_id, order_date);

docs.title: Vector Embedding
docs.text: A numerical representation of text, images, or other data in a high-dimensional space, where semantically similar items cluster close together. Used in search, recommendations, and RAG pipelines.

docs.title: Definition
docs.text: Converts unstructured content into dense numeric arrays (e.g., 1536 dimensions for text-embedding-ada-002).

docs.title: Azure AI Search Setup
docs.text:
from openai import AzureOpenAI

client = AzureOpenAI(
    api_key=os.environ["AZURE_OPENAI_KEY"],
    api_version="2024-02-01",
    azure_endpoint=os.environ["AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT"]
)

def embed(text: str) -> list[float]:
    response = client.embeddings.create(
        input=text,
        model="text-embedding-ada-002"
    )
    return response.data[0].embedding

docs.title: When to Use
docs.text:
• Semantic search over Lakehouse documents
  • Deduplication (cosine similarity threshold ≥ 0.95)
  • RFP retrieval by intent, not keyword

docs.title: 3. docs/notes/data-migration.md
docs.text:
Documenting your AX 2012 to D365 experience here is high-value for recruiters.

```markdown

docs.title: The Migration Framework
docs.text:
• Extraction: Exporting SQL-based schema to Azure Blob Storage using self-hosted Integration Runtimes.
  • Transformation: Mapping legacy fields to the Common Data Service (CDS) / Dataverse model.
  • Validation: Automated reconciliation reports to ensure 100% data fidelity.

docs.title: Lessons Learned
docs.text:
1. Schema Drift: Metadata mapping is the most significant bottleneck.
  2. Performance: Utilize partitioned parallel execution to reduce cutover time during the migration window.

docs.title: Fabric Lakehouse Architecture
docs.text: A unified analytics platform in Microsoft Fabric that combines the flexibility of a data lake with the structure of a data warehouse. Data is stored as Delta Parquet files in OneLake with Bronze/Silver/Gold medallion layers.

docs.title: The Medallion Pattern
docs.text: A robust framework for data organization: * Bronze (Raw): Landing zone, ingested via Data Factory. No transformations applied. * Silver (Cleansed): Data quality checks, deduplication, and schema enforcement (PySpark). * Gold (Curated): Business-ready data modeled as Star Schemas for Power BI.

docs.title: Key Performance Strategy
docs.text:
• Direct Lake Mode: The game changer. Eliminates import latency by querying Parquet files directly in the Lakehouse.
  • Optimization: Always partition by Date and use V-Order to accelerate engine reads.

docs.title: Medallion Architecture
docs.text:
OneLake/
  ├── Bronze/   # Raw ingested data (no transforms)
  ├── Silver/   # Cleaned, joined, typed
  └── Gold/     # Aggregated, business-ready

docs.title: PySpark Write Example
docs.text:
df.write.format("delta") \
    .mode("overwrite") \
    .option("overwriteSchema", "true") \
    .save("abfss://workspace@onelake.dfs.fabric.microsoft.com/lakehouse.Lakehouse/Tables/silver_sales")

docs.title: Key Concepts
docs.text:
• OneLake: Single tenant-wide storage layer (like ADLS Gen2)
  • Shortcuts: Virtual mounts to external data sources
  • Notebooks + Spark: Primary compute for ELT pipelines

docs.title: Project Overview
docs.text:
A 15TB end-to-end data project using Microsoft Fabric.

!!! success "Key Technical Win" Processed 15 terabytes of enterprise data using a three-tier Medallion Architecture.

!!! note "Technical Stack" * Data: PySpark, Fabric Data Factory * AI: MLflow, Auto-ML * Reporting: Power BI Direct Lake

docs.title: The Solution
docs.text: I moved the logic from static fine-tuning to an Agentic RAG workflow, allowing the AI to query the live Gold-layer Lakehouse for real-time demand forecasting.
